# Intact Interview Pack
## Director AI, Chief Data Office | Indhira Mani & Ankur Gupta

> [!tip] The three things they care about above all else: (1) Can you build and own a function from scratch? (2) Are you technically credible enough that Ankur trusts you on his stack? (3) Do you lead with people and business value the way Indhira does?

-----

## What Each Interviewer Is Listening For

### Indhira Mani (CDO) — Your Hiring Manager

- People-first leadership — do you embed with the business or sit in a data tower?
- Pragmatism over perfection — quick wins that make the business say “can you do more of that?”
- Change management — can you bring resistant stakeholders on the AI journey?
- Commercial mindset — do you connect AI to business outcomes, not just technical capability?
- Long-term commitment — are you here to build something enduring?

### Ankur Gupta (Director, Data & ML Products) — Your Peer

- Technical credibility — can you interrogate architecture and challenge external output?
- Platform knowledge — do you know Databricks and Azure AI Foundry properly?
- Boundary clarity — will you complement his ML platform work or step on it?
- Agentic AI depth — do you understand the difference between RAG, LLM apps, and true agentic systems?
- Evaluation rigour — do you build AI systems that can be trusted in a regulated environment?

-----

## Question 1 — Opening (Indhira)

**“Tell us about yourself — why this role, why now, and why Intact specifically.”**

### What They Want to Hear

- Concise and structured — under 90 seconds
- Specific commercial impact numbers from L&G
- A clear articulation of why *owning* a function matters to you personally
- At least one Intact-specific detail that shows you know their story

### Model Answer

“Thank you for having me. I’m an AI and data science leader with a track record of building AI functions from the ground up in regulated financial services. At Legal & General I built a 10-person team, owned a £1M budget, and delivered £1.4M in commercial value — across pricing optimisation, GenAI underwriting, and decision systems — while advising the CEO, CFO, and COO directly on AI strategy. More recently at EY I’m leading agentic AI delivery for energy and utility clients on Databricks and Azure AI Foundry.

The Director AI role is where I’ve been operating, not where I’m heading. The reason Intact is specifically compelling is the combination of a greenfield AI mandate, a CDO who’s building something genuinely new, and a commercial insurance business at a pivotal transformation moment — Guidewire now live, NIG integrated, and a clear ambition to be the UK’s best commercial insurer. I want to build the AI function that makes that happen.”

### Key Points They’re Listening For

- L&G numbers: 10-person team, £1M budget, £1.4M value
- “Where I’ve been operating, not where I’m heading” — confidence without arrogance
- Intact-specific: Guidewire, NIG, CDO, commercial lines ambition
- EY: Databricks and Azure AI Foundry — names the exact stack they use

-----

## Question 2 — 90-Day Plan (Ankur)

**“You’ve talked about building teams and delivering value. But this role is greenfield — no team, no established data products, no AI in production. Walk me through exactly how you’d approach the first 90 days.”**

### What They Want to Hear

- Structured 30/60/90 framework
- Listening and embedding before building — mirrors Indhira’s philosophy
- Explicit mention of partnering with Ankur’s team on the platform
- A specific example of what a quick win might look like
- Evaluation framework defined before anything ships

### Model Answer

“My first 30 days would be entirely about listening and mapping. I’d embed with teams across the full value chain — submission, underwriting, pricing, claims, customer services — to understand where the pain points are and where AI can create the most immediate value. Critically, I’d spend dedicated time with Ankur’s team to understand the data platform, what’s production-ready, what’s in flight, and where the AI CoE can create demand that justifies the platform investments already being made.

By day 30 I’d have a use case register — validated with business unit heads and the CDO — prioritised by three dimensions: business value, technology readiness, and visibility. I’d select two or three short-term high-value initiatives alongside one or two strategic long-term objectives.

By day 60 I’d have the first hire or two in motion, the tech stack agreed with Ankur’s team, and our evaluation framework defined — so when we ship something, we can measure it properly. I’d also have a first POC in development, likely a document intelligence or submission triage capability.

By day 90 I’d have delivered at least one proof of concept that the business can see and react to. That early win isn’t just about the technology — it’s about establishing trust, showing the CDO team what the AI function can do, and creating the appetite to scale.”

### Key Points They’re Listening For

- Listening before building — day one is people, not platforms
- Explicit Ankur partnership — “understand the platform before building on it”
- Prioritisation criteria: value, readiness, visibility
- Evaluation framework before production — shows engineering maturity
- Quick win within 90 days — creates internal momentum

-----

## Question 3 — Long-Term Commitment (Indhira)

**“You’ve been at EY for just over two months. Why should we believe this isn’t just a stepping stone?”**

### What They Want to Hear

- Direct acknowledgement of the short tenure — don’t dodge it
- A clear distinction between consulting and in-house ownership
- Personal anchor — why owning a function matters to *you*
- Confidence in the final line — not defensive

### Model Answer

“EY has been genuinely valuable — the exposure to different industries and delivery models has sharpened how I think. But consulting at senior levels becomes increasingly about winning work, not building things. That’s not where I create the most impact.

At L&G I built something from nothing — a team, a function, a pipeline of AI systems — and I saw what it felt like when that compounds over time. I left before I could fully see it mature, and that stayed with me. Intact is the opportunity to do that properly — build the AI function, stay long enough to see it become a genuine competitive advantage for the business, and grow with it.

Two months at EY doesn’t worry me — it tells me I know quickly when something isn’t the right long-term fit. This is.”

### Key Points They’re Listening For

- Consulting vs. in-house distinction — clear and genuine
